text.title: Product Manager Agents — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for using the two product-scoping subagents (faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent + faion-mlp-agent) so MVP→MLP work stops being a vibes-led roadmap session and starts being a traceable pipeline.

rule.statement: Run faion-mlp-agent modes in strict sequence: analyze → find-gaps → propose → update → plan. Never skip a mode. Each mode's output is the required input of the next.
rule.rationale: Each mode's output is the required input for the next. Gap analysis without a current-state baseline produces hallucinated gaps; spec updates without gap validation produce incorrect MLP requirements.

rule.statement: A product manager MUST review and select which WOW features to accept after mode:propose, BEFORE running mode:update. mode:update writes to spec files autonomously.

rationale.source: Human-in-the-loop guidance for autonomous file edits

rule.rationale: If the agent misinterprets a gap or proposes an incorrect MLP feature, the spec is modified without a review step. Chaining propose to update without human approval produces unreviewed spec changes.

rule.statement: Spec files MUST be committed to version control BEFORE running mode:update. The agent modifies files in place; uncommitted changes are unreviewable.

rationale.source: Auditability requirement for autonomous file edits

rule.rationale: Without version control, changes are not reviewable as a diff and are effectively unauditable.

rule.statement: faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent MUST be given ≥3 named comparable products. Running it without comparables produces hallucinated feature floors.
rule.rationale: Competitor feature analysis provides an empirical floor for the minimum feature set. Without it, the agent either under-builds (no table stakes) or over-builds (scope creep on non-differentiating features).

rule.statement: For B2B products, weight Trust and Ease higher than Delight and Personality when running faion-mlp-agent. Cookie-cutter MLP dimensions do not apply without modification.
rule.rationale: Enterprise buyers optimise for reliability and onboarding speed, not emotional resonance. Equal-weight MLP dimensions mis-rank features for B2B contexts.

example.code: # Net-new product without an existing MVP Task(subagent_type="faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent", prompt="Analyze SaaS PM tools for MVP scope. Target: small teams (5-15), focus on task tracking + collab. Comparables: Linear, Jira, Notion.") # Then hand the output to faion-mlp-agent starting at mode:propose. Task(subagent_type="faion-mlp-agent", prompt="mode: propose. Input: <scope-analyzer output>. Dimensions: ease, speed (B2B weights).")
Task(subagent_type="faion-mlp-agent", prompt="mode: update. Improve this spec.") # skipped analyze + find-gaps + propose
example.note: Skipping modes produces incorrect MLP requirements; running mode:update without human-reviewed mode:propose corrupts spec files autonomously.
